session_label: Discovery-completeness closure of 6 residual gaps from 2023 Digital Orientalist article
scope: Per-portal Cihanşah / Hakîkî / Yusuf Hakîkî query battery against the 6 residual gaps documented in crosscheck_2026-05-11_digital_orientalist_2023_resources.json: Vienna ONB Sondersammlungen, Berlin Staatsbibliothek Digital Library, MENA-DOC / MENAlib Halle, Bologna historica.unibo.it, HathiTrust, and Chester Beatty viewer.cbl.ie. Discovery-completeness goal: actual full-battery query against each portal's catalogue interface, with any non-zero hit verified to either confirm a new witness candidate or document the false-positive class.
geopolitical_caveat: Not applicable; all 6 portals are in Western countries unaffected by Iranian wartime infrastructure shutdowns.

method_summary.tier_1_query_battery: Multi-variant Cihanşah query battery per portal including Latin transliterations (Jahanshah, Cihanshah, Hakiki, Haqiqi, Yusuf Hakiki, Karakoyunlu, Qaraqoyunlu), Persian script (جهانشاه, حقیقی), local-language Orientalist variants (German Dschihanschah / Dschehanschah, Italian Gianschah / Caraqoyunlu), and broad context terms (Aq Qoyunlu, Black Sheep Turkoman).
method_summary.tier_2_js_spa_handling: Playwright with full DOM rendering and 2-5 second post-navigation wait. URL-parameter probes attempted first; SPA form-submission via DOM manipulation fallback when URL params didn't filter (Berlin Stabi case: discovered queryString= parameter is the active filter; Vienna ONB Primo accepts ?query= directly; Bologna AMS Historica accepts ?query= directly).
method_summary.tier_3_anubis_handling: Chester Beatty viewer.cbl.ie uses Anubis JS proof-of-work challenge. Initial project memory documented Anubis as 'blocks headless browsers'. Empirical finding: Anubis is solvable by patient headless Chromium given 10-15 seconds per fresh navigation for the PoW computation to complete; the block is fast-scanning-only, not headless-categorical. Rapid back-to-back queries trigger re-challenge; sequential queries with 12-15s waits succeed.
method_summary.tier_4_captcha_cloudflare_fallback: When portal catalogue returns CAPTCHA or persistent Cloudflare 'Just a moment' challenge unresolvable by headless (MENA-DOC opendata.uni-halle.de DSpace; HathiTrust catalog.hathitrust.org), pivot to site-restricted Google search (site:domain Cihanşah Hakîkî variants) to surface any indexed catalogue records via Google's prior crawl. Strong negative evidence if Google returns zero relevant matches given the comprehensiveness of Google's coverage of these portals.

vienna_onb.method: Playwright URL-parameter navigation against the Primo search endpoint
vienna_onb.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. 8 of 8 variants returned zero. Caveat: the Primo interface notes 'Sign in to get complete results and to request items'; there could be member-only records not surfaced to public search, but no evidence either way.
non_web_access_paths: Direct correspondence with ONB Sondersammlungen if v2.0 chancery scope wants definitive answer including member-only records

berlin_staatsbibliothek.endpoint: https://digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/suche (SPA, queryString= parameter; URL pattern locked via form-submit DOM manipulation)
berlin_staatsbibliothek.method: Playwright with batched URL-parameter navigation; non-zero hits investigated for false-positive identification

queries_executed.outcome: 2 Suchergebnisse, ALL coincidental compound matches (see false_positive_class_2)
1 Suchergebnis, Burton 1851 Sindh ethnography compound-match (see false_positive_class_2)

false_positive_classes.class_1_vohd_adjective_matches: 10 of 13 non-zero hits across Haqiqi + Qaraqoyunlu queries are volumes of the Verzeichnis der orientalischen Handschriften in Deutschland (VOHD) series, the German national union catalogue of oriental manuscripts. Matches are fulltext body-text matches on the Arabic adjective حقیقی ('true/real') in catalogue descriptions of OTHER manuscripts (e.g., 'al-kufr al-haqiqi' = 'real disbelief' in a theological treatise; 'al-faqir al-haqiqi' = scribe self-deprecation formula). CRITICAL NEGATIVE EVIDENCE: VOHD Band 13 Teil 1 (Flemming 1968) and Teil 4 (Götz 1979) are the authoritative Berlin Persian manuscript catalogues. If Cihanşah were in Berlin's collection, VOHD would index him as named author, not as fulltext-only keyword.
false_positive_classes.class_2_coincidental_compound_matches: 3 of 13 non-zero hits are unrelated works that happen to contain matching strings: Schirokauer 1914 'Die siebente Großmacht' (WW1 novel mentioning 'Osmanli Mudafai Hakiki' = Ottoman National Defense Society 'Hakiki'/true); Burton 1851 'Sindh' colonial-era ethnography mentioning 'Kufr i Hakiki' (true infidelity) and 'Hajjaj bin Yusuf' (a separate 7th-c. governor of Iraq).

berlin_staatsbibliothek.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. 12 variants tested, 13 non-zero hits all attributable to documented false-positive classes; zero actual Cihanşah Hakîkî witnesses. Strong negative evidence reinforced by VOHD indexing exhaustiveness for Berlin Persian collection.
non_web_access_paths: Future v2.0 might consult Pertsch's 19th-c. Berlin Persian manuscripts catalogue digitally for any historical Hakîkî reference that postdates VOHD coverage

queries_executed.outcome: DSpace backend at opendata.uni-halle.de returned the CAPTCHA challenge page 'Share_it: Are you a robot?'; automated query unresolvable; this is a DSpace anti-bot wrapper distinct from the MENAlib landing page

site:opendata.uni-halle.de Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu Hakiki Haqiqi manuscript returned 10 PDFs none of which are MENAdoc manuscript records; all matches are loose body-text matches in scholarly papers (Schmidt Leiden Turkish manuscripts catalogue, Peacock-Yıldız 2016 'Islamic Literature in 14th-15th c. Anatolia', misc. Islamic studies PDFs); none indexes Cihanşah as a Hakîkî witness

mena_doc_halle.definitive_verdict: RESIDUAL-CAPTCHA functionally exhausted via Google site-search. The DSpace backend at opendata.uni-halle.de is CAPTCHA-protected for automated queries; the public-facing MENAdoc landing has no working URL-parameter search; site-restricted Google sweep across both the user-facing and indexing domains returned ZERO manuscript records relevant to Cihanşah / Hakîkî / Karakoyunlu. Given the comprehensiveness of Google's crawl of Halle's open-access repositories, this is a strong negative.
non_web_access_paths: Headed-browser session with manual CAPTCHA solving for direct DSpace catalogue search (low yield expected given Google evidence)

Note for v2.0: the Peacock-Yıldız 2016 'Islamic Literature in 14th and 15th-century Anatolia' volume identified via the site:opendata search may contain Cihanşah-related historiographic references; worth backchain-mining for chancery / inscription corpus extension

bologna_historica.endpoint: https://historica.unibo.it/search (AMS Historica = Bologna University digital library; DSpace-GLAM backend; ?query= URL parameter)
bologna_historica.method: Playwright URL-parameter batched navigation (post-Retry-After rate-limit window)
bologna_historica.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE. 11 of 11 variants returned zero. Confirms the article's characterization of Bologna's Arabic manuscripts collection as small (~450 mss) with 'some Persian' and no Cihanşah relevance.
non_web_access_paths: Direct correspondence with Bologna University Library if v2.0 wants definitive coverage; very low priority given collection scope

hathitrust.method: Playwright navigation hit Cloudflare 'Just a moment' challenge that did not auto-resolve within 8 seconds; pivoted to site-restricted Google sweep as fallback

queries_executed.query: Direct Cloudflare-protected catalog.hathitrust.org navigation for Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu
queries_executed.outcome: Cloudflare challenge persistent; headless does not pass

queries_executed.query: site:catalog.hathitrust.org Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu OR Cihanshah OR Hakiki manuscript Persian
site:babel.hathitrust.org Jahanshah Hakiki Qaraqoyunlu Persian manuscript
queries_executed.outcome: 10 results returned, all are general HathiTrust collection-landing pages (Islamic Manuscripts Michigan collection index, Manuscript Collections University of Illinois index, Hakluyt Society Series, ASOR Collection, etc.); none is a specific Cihanşah-attributed manuscript record

hathitrust.definitive_verdict: RESIDUAL-CLOUDFLARE functionally exhausted via Google site-search. Live catalogue blocked to headless by Cloudflare 'Just a moment'; site-restricted Google sweep across both catalog.hathitrust.org and babel.hathitrust.org returned ZERO Cihanşah-specific manuscript records (only general collection-landing pages). Given Google's comprehensive crawl of HathiTrust public records, this is a strong negative.
non_web_access_paths: Headed-browser session with manual Cloudflare bypass for live catalogue search (low yield expected)

HathiTrust catalogue records are largely aggregated from US research libraries already covered by Fihrist (UK) and DLME aggregators probed in v1.0; Fihrist is the source-of-truth for Persian manuscript discoverability

chester_beatty.method: Playwright navigation with 10-15 second waits per fresh navigation to allow Anubis PoW to complete; rapid sequential queries trigger Anubis re-challenge

queries_executed.outcome: Same Pir Budaq majmua record (CBL Per 134)
29 hits, ALL Prophet-Yusuf (Joseph) Quranic/Biblical references; see false_positive_class_yusuf

queries_executed.query: Black Sheep Turkoman (English literal of Qaraqoyunlu)
queries_executed.outcome: 133 hits, broad body-text matches across CBL catalogue descriptions; too noisy for direct Cihanşah relevance

pir_budaq_majmua_finding.title: Collection (majmū`) of 4 literary texts, for Pīr Būdāq (d. 1466)
pir_budaq_majmua_finding.materials: Codex, ink, colours and gold on paper
pir_budaq_majmua_finding.decoration: Illuminated medallion (shamsa) on fol. 1r; four headings (sarlawh) on fols. 1v, 11v, 36v, 46v
pir_budaq_majmua_finding.dedication: bi-rasm khizānat al-sulṭān (for the treasury of the sultan); Qaraqoyunlu ruler Abū al-Fatḥ Pīr Būdāq Bahādur Khān (d. 1466)

four_texts_inventory.title: Ḥikāya-ye gol o mol (Discourse of the rose and grapevine)
four_texts_inventory.author: Muḥammad Zangī Bukhārī (same)

four_texts_inventory.title: Risāla-ye radd-e shaṭranj (On the refutation of chess [by backgammon])
four_texts_inventory.author: Ḥusām ibn Muḥammad Rashīd Ṣarrāf Khwārazmī

pir_budaq_majmua_finding.cihansah_witness_status: NOT a Cihanşah Hakîkî witness. The 4 texts are all by pre-Qaraqoyunlu authors (Marāghī d.1266, Bukhārī, Khwārazmī); none of them is Cihanşah Hakîkî. The Qaraqoyunlu-related search hit is triggered by the dedication formula 'for the treasury of Pīr Būdāq Bahādur Khān (d. 1466)'; Pir Budaq was Jahanshah Qaraqoyunlu's son.
pir_budaq_majmua_finding.v1_comparator_corpus_relevance: HIGH. CBL Per 134 is a new addition to the v1.0 Pir Budaq atelier comparator-corpus (which previously documented Christie's 2019/2024 Pir Budaq + Sotheby's 2020 Pir Budaq Hafiz + Sotheby's 2015 Aq Qoyunlu Nava'i). Per 134's codicology (Shiraz 1459-1460, Shaykh Maḥmūd calligrapher, illuminated shamsa + 4 sarlawh, ink+colours+gold on paper, nasta`liq) is exactly the kind of Pir Budaq atelier features the v1.0 BL Or 9493 decoration diagnostic compared against. Per 134 does NOT change the v1.0 BL Or 9493 NEGATIVE triangulation (which was already strong: 4 examiners + 3 prior Pir Budaq comparators), but it strengthens the comparator-corpus characterization of Pir Budaq atelier features.
pir_budaq_majmua_finding.v2_chancery_scope_relevance: MODERATE. Per 134's dedication medallion uses the formula 'bi-rasm khizānat al-sulṭān...' which is a chancery-document convention (manuscripts produced for sultans' treasury libraries). For v2.0 chancery scope this is a known pattern, not a new finding.

chester_beatty.false_positive_class_yusuf: 29 hits on 'Yusuf Hakiki' all reference Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) of Quranic/Biblical stories. Sample titles: 'Portraits of the Prophets Yūsuf, Ayyūb...', 'The Prophets Ya'qub and Yusuf seated together', 'The older Zulaykhā meets Yūsuf passing her home' (from Yūsuf u Zulaykhā by Ḥamdullāh Chelebī d.1509). Compound match: 'Yusuf' (Joseph) appears in titles + 'Hakiki' may appear in fulltext catalogue descriptions or paratext. None refers to the poet Yusuf Hakîkî.
chester_beatty.definitive_verdict: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE for Cihanşah Hakîkî witness. 11 variants probed. The 1 Qaraqoyunlu-tagged record (CBL Per 134 = Pir Budaq majmua) is NOT a Hakîkî witness but adds to the v1.0 Pir Budaq atelier comparator-corpus. The 29 Yusuf-Hakiki hits are all Prophet-Joseph false positives. The 133 Black-Sheep-Turkoman hits are too-broad body-text matches.
chester_beatty.anubis_methodology_lesson: Anubis JS-PoW challenge IS solvable by patient headless Chromium given 10-15 seconds per fresh navigation. Project memory previously documented Anubis as 'blocks headless browsers'; empirical correction: Anubis blocks fast-scanning headless but not patient headless. Rapid back-to-back queries trigger re-challenge; sequential queries with 12-15s waits succeed. This is a useful finding for the v2.0 toolkit and for future CBL searches.

summary.comparator_corpus_addition_detail: CBL Per 134; Pir Budaq Mirza majmua, Shiraz 1459-1460 by Shaykh Maḥmūd; added to v1.0 Pir Budaq atelier diagnostic comparator set. Does not change the v1.0 BL Or 9493 NEGATIVE triangulation but strengthens characterization of Pir Budaq atelier features.

definitive_verdicts.berlin_staatsbibliothek: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE (12 variants tested, 13 false-positive hits all explained, zero Cihanşah witnesses)
definitive_verdicts.mena_doc_halle: RESIDUAL-CAPTCHA functionally exhausted via Google site-search (zero relevant)
definitive_verdicts.hathitrust: RESIDUAL-CLOUDFLARE functionally exhausted via Google site-search (zero relevant)
definitive_verdicts.chester_beatty: EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE for Hakîkî witness (1 Qaraqoyunlu hit = Pir Budaq majmua, NOT Hakîkî; +1 new comparator-corpus addition)

structural_findings: All 6 residual gaps from the 2023 Digital Orientalist article are now closed against the Cihanşah / Hakîkî witness question. Four are EXHAUSTED-NEGATIVE via direct catalogue search (Vienna, Berlin, Bologna, CBL); two are RESIDUAL with respect to live-catalogue access (MENA-DOC CAPTCHA; HathiTrust Cloudflare) but functionally negative via Google site-restricted search.

Multi-variant query batteries (8-12 variants per portal, including local-language Orientalist transliterations) materially expanded coverage rigor over single-query probing. German Orientalist Dschihanschah and Italian Caraqoyunlu were tested and returned zero; this rules out the hypothesis that historical local-language catalogues might index Cihanşah under non-standard orthography.

False-positive classes documented for outward-facing audit-trail: (a) VOHD-fulltext-adjective-matches at Berlin (Arabic adjective حقیقی in catalogue descriptions of OTHER manuscripts); (b) coincidental-compound-matches at Berlin (Burton 1851 Sindh ethnography, Schirokauer 1914 novel); (c) Prophet-Yusuf-Quranic-matches at CBL (29 hits on 'Yusuf Hakiki' all referring to Prophet Joseph); (d) too-broad-historical-context-matches at CBL (133 'Black Sheep Turkoman' body-text matches).

Anubis JS-PoW challenge at CBL is solvable by patient headless Chromium with 12-15s waits per fresh navigation; a useful methodology refinement vs. the prior project-memory note 'Anubis blocks headless browsers'. Captured for v2.0 toolkit reuse.

Site-restricted Google search (site:domain query) is a useful fallback for portals whose live catalogues are CAPTCHA or Cloudflare protected, providing strong negative evidence when Google has comprehensively crawled the public records. Used to functionally close MENA-DOC and HathiTrust without bypassing their anti-bot.
summary.register_changes: none; witness_register.json is unchanged. The 14-entry register persists. The CBL Per 134 finding is comparator-corpus addition, not register addition.
summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO new witnesses. The v1.0 14-entry register is structurally unchanged.
summary.v1_comparator_corpus_impact: MODERATE addition: CBL Per 134 added to the Pir Budaq atelier diagnostic comparator set used by the v1.0 BL Or 9493 NEGATIVE triangulation. The triangulation's conclusion is unchanged but its underlying comparator-corpus is now better characterized.
summary.v2_scope_impact: Minor: Per 134's chancery-formula dedication ('bi-rasm khizānat al-sulṭān') is a known pattern; the Peacock-Yıldız 2016 Anatolian Islamic literature volume surfaced via the MENA-DOC site:Google sweep is worth backchain-mining for v2.0 chancery / historiographic scope.
follow_on_targets_for_future_session: v1.0 register: NONE; no changes warranted
v1.0 comparator-corpus: ADD CBL Per 134 to the Pir Budaq atelier comparator set with full codicology (cited above) as an additional comparison point for the BL Or 9493 decoration triangulation

v2.0 scope: backchain-mine Peacock-Yıldız 2016 'Islamic Literature and Intellectual Life in Fourteenth- and Fifteenth-Century Anatolia' for any Cihanşah / Qaraqoyunlu chancery / historiographic references

Future re-probe (low priority): headed-browser pass at MENA-DOC opendata.uni-halle.de and HathiTrust catalog.hathitrust.org to verify the Google-sweep negative against live catalogue search; expected yield ~zero
Anubis methodology: document the 12-15s-patient-headless pattern for v2.0 reuse and update project memory accordingly
